root.narration: The ball is bouncing toward your strong-hand side. You have time to set up for a forehand. How do you swing?

choices.text: Turn sideways, pull your racket back, step forward, and swing through the ball finishing with your racket up high
Face the net and swing your arm at the ball without turning your body
Stand still and just stick your racket out hoping the ball hits it

outcome.headline: Beautiful forehand!
outcome.explanation: By turning sideways and stepping into the ball, you used your whole body to create power. Finishing with the racket high sent the ball over the net with control.
outcome.whatToRemember: A good forehand starts with turning your body sideways. Step into the ball and follow through high over your opposite shoulder.

keyTerms.definition: Continuing your swing after you hit the ball, finishing with the racket up high over your opposite shoulder for control and power

outcome.headline: You got the ball back, but without power!
outcome.explanation: Facing the net and swinging only with your arm means the ball goes back softly. Your opponent will have an easy time hitting a strong return.
outcome.whatToRemember: Turn your body sideways before you swing. Your legs and hips give you way more power than just your arm.

outcome.headline: The ball bounced right past you!
outcome.explanation: Just sticking your racket out without swinging gives you no control. The ball hits the frame and flies off in a random direction, landing out of bounds.
outcome.whatToRemember: Always swing through the ball. Standing still and reaching is not a real shot -- move your feet and swing with purpose.
